methods.name: Clear
methods.signature: public void Clear()
methods.summary: Marks all items as deleted.

methods.name: Compact
methods.signature: public void Compact(bool ignoreUndoReferences)
methods.summary: Purge deleted instance definition information that is not in use. This function is time consuming and should be used in a thoughtful manner.

params.name: ignoreUndoReferences
params.doc: If false, then deleted instance definition information that could possibly be undeleted by the Undo command will not be deleted. If true, then all deleted instance definition information is deleted.

methods.name: Delete(InstanceDefinition)
methods.signature: public override bool Delete(InstanceDefinition item)
methods.summary: Deletes the instance definition. This deletes all references too.

params.name: item
params.doc: The item to delete.

returns.doc: True on success.

methods.name: Delete(Int32, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Delete(int idefIndex, bool deleteReferences, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Deletes the instance definition.

params.name: idefIndex
params.doc: zero based index of instance definition to delete. This must be in the range 0 <= idefIndex < InstanceDefinitionTable.Count.

params.name: deleteReferences
params.doc: true to delete all references to this definition. false to delete definition only if there are no references.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, no warning message box appears if an instance definition cannot be deleted because it is the current layer or it contains active geometry.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if the instance definition has active references and bDeleteReferences is false.

methods.name: DestroySourceArchive
methods.signature: public bool DestroySourceArchive(InstanceDefinition definition, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Destroys all source archive information. Specifically: * SourceArchive is set to the empty string. * SourceRelativePath is set to false * The alternative source archive path is set to the empty string. * Checksum.Zero() is used to private destroy all checksum information. * UpdateType is set to Static.

params.name: definition
params.doc: The instance definition to be modified.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, then message boxes about erroneous parameters will not be shown.

returns.doc: Returns true if the definition was successfully modified otherwise returns false.

methods.summary: Finds the instance definition with a given name.

params.name: instanceDefinitionName
params.doc: name of instance definition to search for (ignores case)

returns.doc: The specified instance definition, or null if nothing matching was found.

params.name: ignoreDeletedInstanceDefinitions
params.doc: true means don't search deleted instance definitions.

returns.doc: The specified instance definition, or null if nothing matching was found.

methods.name: Find(String, Boolean)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("ignoreDeletedInstanceDefinitions is now redundant. Remove the second argument. Definitions are now always deleted permanently.")] public InstanceDefinition Find(string instanceDefinitionName, bool ignoreDeletedInstanceDefinitions)
methods.summary: Finds the instance definition with a given name.

params.name: instanceDefinitionName
params.doc: name of instance definition to search for (ignores case)

params.name: ignoreDeletedInstanceDefinitions
params.doc: true means don't search deleted instance definitions.

returns.doc: The specified instance definition, or null if nothing matching was found.

methods.name: FindId
methods.signature: public virtual T FindId(Guid id)
methods.summary: Uses the guid to find a model component. Deleted objects cannot be found by id. The guid is the value that is stored in the .Id property. In a single document, no two active objects have the same guid. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the guid persists. For example, if the _Move command moves an object, then the moved object inherits its guid from the starting object. If the Copy command copies an object, then the copy gets a new guid. This guid persists through file saving/opening operations. This function will not find grip objects.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of model component to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.

params.name: ignoreDeleted
params.doc: If true then deleted instance definitions are filtered out.

returns.doc: An array of instance definitions. This can be empty, but not null.

methods.name: GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName()
methods.signature: public string GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName()
methods.summary: Gets unused instance definition name used as default when creating new instance definitions.

returns.doc: An unused instance definition name string.

methods.name: GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName(String)
methods.signature: public string GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName(string root)
methods.summary: Gets unused instance definition name used as default when creating new instance definitions.

params.name: root
params.doc: The returned name is 'root nn' If root is empty, then 'Block' (localized) is used.

returns.doc: An unused instance definition name string.

methods.name: GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName(String, UInt32)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("The defaultSuffix parameter is now ignored. Remove the second argument.")] public string GetUnusedInstanceDefinitionName(string root, uint defaultSuffix)
methods.summary: Gets unused instance definition name used as default when creating new instance definitions.

params.name: root
params.doc: The returned name is 'root nn' If root is empty, then 'Block' (localized) is used.

params.name: defaultSuffix
params.doc: Unique names are created by appending a decimal number to the localized term for "Block" as in "Block 01", "Block 02", and so on. When defaultSuffix is supplied, the search for an unused name begins at "Block suffix".

returns.doc: An unused instance definition name string.

params.name: instanceId
params.doc: Unique id of the instance definition to search for

params.name: ignoreDeletedInstanceDefinitions
params.doc: true means don't search deleted instance definitions.

returns.doc: index > -1 if instance definition was found.

methods.name: MakeSourcePathRelative
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Source paths are always absolute at runtime. They cannot be changed.")] public bool MakeSourcePathRelative(InstanceDefinition idef, bool relative, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Obsolete method that always returns false. Marks the source path for a linked instance definition as relative or absolute.

params.name: idef
params.doc: The instance definition to be marked.

params.name: relative
params.doc: If true, the path should be considered as relative.If false, the path should be considered as absolute.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, then message boxes about erroneous parameters will not be shown.

returns.doc: true if the instance definition could be modified.

methods.name: UpdateLinkedInstanceDefinition
methods.signature: public bool UpdateLinkedInstanceDefinition(int idefIndex, string filename, bool updateNestedLinks, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Read the objects from a file and use them as the instance's definition geometry.

params.name: idefIndex
params.doc: zero based index of instance definition to delete. This must be in the range 0 <= idefIndex < InstanceDefinitionTable.Count.

params.name: filename
params.doc: name of file (can be any type of file that Rhino or a plug-in can read)

params.name: updateNestedLinks
params.doc: If true and the instance definition references to a linked instance definition, that needs to be updated, then the nested definition is also updated. If false, nested updates are skipped.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: [Missing <param name="quiet"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.InstanceDefinitionTable.UpdateLinkedInstanceDefinition(System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)"]

properties.name: ActiveCount
properties.summary: Number of items in the instance definitions table, excluding deleted definitions.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Number of items in the instance definitions table.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Conceptually, the InstanceDefinition table is an array of Instance definitions. The operator[] can be used to get individual instance definition. An instance definition is either active or deleted and this state is reported by IsDeleted or will be null if it has been purged from the document.
